title: [EPIC] Deterministic evaluator & operator realisation
description: SPEC Â§Evaluation model, Â§Modes, Â§Config operators. Bottom-up evaluator + the non-LLM realisation strategies so the whole `tests:` block passes offline. Child scope (label `eval`): DAG evaluator (operand-first); realisation resolution (`command`/`reduce`/`template`/`join`/`model`) + mode selection; template realisation (`%`, `%0`/`%1`, `%%`); join realisation (variadic); numeric `reduce` builtins (add/sub/mul/div/pow); command realisation (`NLIR_ARGS`, stdout); operand type coercion before apply; list rendering via `_sep` + spread into variadic ops; nullary-pop stack consumption.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: CI: self-hosted release workflow on v* tag push
description: Parent epic bd-e0a557 (label `release`). `.github/workflows/release.yml` on self-hosted runner(s), triggered by `v*` tags: build `nlir` binaries via the flake (linux x86_64/aarch64, darwin arm64 as applicable) plus `.sha256` sidecars, stream assets straight into a DRAFT GitHub release, then publish after an asset-inspection gate confirms the expected binary set (the stream-to-draft-then-inspect pattern). Mirror `omni-cli`/`tendril`/`kittui`/`ring-mods` `release.yml`; see the cacophony SPEC release section for the streaming/inspection rationale. Keep runners self-hosted with the correct labels.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

description: Parent epic bd-c46071 (label `lexer`). After `^`/`$`, `* _ /` are role modifiers and leading `-` is a negative index (not subtract).

title: CLI: command tree & top-level flags

title: Project skeleton: Nix flake + Rust `nlir` CLI with crate deps wired
description:
Foundational bead. `SPEC.md` at the repo root is the normative contract for the whole project â€” read it first; all beads implement it.

Set up the initial project scaffolding so every downstream bead has somewhere to land:

- **Nix flake** managing a Rust CLI, following the same pattern as the other harryaskham Rust CLIs. Use the **`omni-cli`** project as the primary reference for the flake + CLI structure (mature examples also live under `~/.cacophony/daemon/checkouts/â€¦`, e.g. `tendril`, `kittui`).
- **Rust binary crate `nlir`** with the command-tree scaffolding and stub subcommands from SPEC Â§CLI surface: `-e`, `parse`, `test`, `repl`, `set`, `get`, `append-message`, `mcp stdio`, `self-update`, `feedback`, plus global flags (`--config`, `--context-file`, `--session-file`, `--mode`, `--model`, `--parallelism`, `--quiet`, `--dry-run`).
- **Wire crate dependencies on the friend-project template stack** (these are configured Cacophony friend projects / sub-crates):
  - `mcp-cli` â€” gives the MCP server almost for free (`nlir mcp stdio`).
  - `updatable-cli` â€” `nlir self-update`.
  - `feedback-cli` â€” `nlir feedback`.
  Reference how `omni-cli` consumes these three crates and mirror that wiring.

Acceptance: `nix build`, `nix flake check`, and `cargo build` are green; `nlir --help` lists the command tree; the three template subcommands are wired (even if their handlers are thin) so later beads only fill in behaviour.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Eval: operand type coercion before apply
description: Parent epic bd-2b226d (label `eval`). Coerce each operand to the operator's declared `operands:` type.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] CI, release & distribution
description: Self-hosted CI on version-tag pushes, release binaries, end-to-end `nlir self-update`/`feedback` wiring, and a self-hosted GitHub Pages site. All patterns are proven in the sibling harryaskham Rust CLIs â€” reference `omni-cli`, `tendril`, `kittui`, `ring-mods` (release + self-update + feedback + pages) and `cacophony` (docs Pages).
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Output: --dry-run
description: Parent epic bd-6cdfee (label `output`). Print DAG + assembled prompts, no calls.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Messages: index resolution
description: Parent epic bd-93dd1a (label `messages`). Indices are expressions coerced to number; negatives from the end.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: LLM: NLIR_ var assembly
description: Parent epic bd-b71b0b (label `llm`). Filled prompt -> `NLIR_PROMPT`; substitute `${NLIR_*}` in messages/command.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Types: LLM coercion fallback
description: Parent epic bd-957ff4 (label `types`). Per-type `types:` model+prompt with `{result:T}` schema when deterministic parse fails.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Parser: prefix/postfix/infix + arity
description: Parent epic bd-ab25f7 (label `parser`). Fixity + arity; `!a&b&c`=`(!a)&b&c`; postfix `?` binds leftward.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Release: wire updatable-cli end-to-end so `nlir self-update` / `nlir update` installs the latest tagged release
description: Parent epic bd-e0a557 (label `release`). Completes the plumbing stub bd-1b0283 and makes the whole update path actually work: point updatable-cli at `harryaskham/nlir` with asset names matching the release workflow, then version-check + download + sha256-verify + atomic stage-`nlir_next`-then-promote install, and verify `nlir self-update` (and the `update` alias if exposed) upgrades onto a real published tag. Reference `omni-cli`/`tendril`/`ring-mods` self-update wiring (the `*_self_update_*` surfaces). Depends on the release-workflow bead (needs published assets to update onto).
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: LLM: model resolution
description: Parent epic bd-b71b0b (label `llm`). Resolve operator `model:` alias; fall back to `defaults.model`/`--model`.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: LLM: command backend
description: Parent epic bd-b71b0b (label `llm`). Subprocess `type: command`; `format` json (result_field) or text (whole stdout).
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: CLI: get subcommand
description: Parent epic bd-bc848a (label `cli`). `get KEY` prints the context value.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Parallelism: backtick forced-serial subtree
description: Parent epic bd-a32894 (label `parallelism`). Backtick subtree serial while parallel w.r.t. siblings.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: CLI: context-source precedence
description: Parent epic bd-bc848a (label `cli`). `--context-file` > `--session-file` > env > default file.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Messages: role-filtered views
description: Parent epic bd-93dd1a (label `messages`). `^`=assistant, `^_`=user, `^*`=all, `^/`=system from config views.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Eval: numeric reduce builtins
description: Parent epic bd-2b226d (label `eval`). add/sub/mul/div/pow over number operands (`+ * / - **`).
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Context: system keys + defaults
description: Parent epic bd-7a1d2f (label `context`). `_messages`, `_sep` (`\n`), `_cache` (true).
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

bead.title: Config: serde schema structs for the full config tree
bead.description: Parent epic bd-b342fd (label `config`). SPEC Â§Example config.yaml. Define serde types for the full config tree: `defaults`, `models`, `prompts`, `operators`, `context`, `sessions`, `types`, `tests`. Foundation the rest of config builds on.
bead.creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham
